name: Sauti za Busara
shortDescription: African music, beach energy and Swahili culture.
description: East Africa's best-known music festival celebrating Swahili culture and African music with performances across Stone Town.

logistics.transportInfo: Best reached by taxi or walking from Stone Town hotels.

faqs.question: Can I re-enter the venue?
faqs.answer: Yes, re-entry is allowed with your wristband.

faqs.question: Are outside drinks allowed?
faqs.answer: No outside beverages permitted.

name: Cape Town International Jazz Festival
shortDescription: World-class jazz over two iconic Cape Town nights.
description: Major international jazz event and one of Africa's best-known urban festivals featuring local and global jazz legends across multiple stages.

logistics.transportInfo: Reachable by taxi, rideshare, or walking from nearby hotels.

faqs.question: Are children allowed?
faqs.answer: Attendees must be 18+ after 8PM.

faqs.question: Is food available?
faqs.answer: Yes, multiple food vendors are on-site.

name: Marrakech Popular Arts Festival
shortDescription: Traditional Moroccan music, dance and street arts.
description: Traditional music, storytelling, dance, and performance art across Marrakech's historic squares and palaces.

logistics.transportInfo: Reachable by taxi, rideshare, or walking from nearby hotels.

faqs.question: Is the festival free?
faqs.answer: Some performances are free; others require tickets.

faqs.question: Best time to attend?
faqs.answer: Evenings are coolest and most lively.

name: Kirstenbosch Summer Concerts
shortDescription: Sunset picnic concerts in a botanical paradise.
description: Outdoor concert series in Cape Town's botanical gardens with sunset energy and picnic-friendly crowds.

logistics.transportInfo: Reachable by taxi, rideshare, or walking from nearby hotels.

faqs.question: Can I bring food?
faqs.answer: Yes, picnic baskets are welcome.

faqs.question: Is alcohol allowed?
faqs.answer: Wine and beer are permitted, no spirits.

name: Zanzibar International Film Festival
shortDescription: Swahili and African cinema by the sea.
description: Swahili and African cinema, live performances, and waterfront cultural energy across Stone Town venues.

logistics.transportInfo: Reachable by taxi, rideshare, or walking from nearby hotels.

faqs.question: Are screenings in English?
faqs.answer: Most have English subtitles.

faqs.question: Are passes available?
faqs.answer: Yes, full festival passes are sold online.

name: Victoria Falls Carnival Week
shortDescription: End-of-year music, adventure and falls.
description: An end-of-year celebration combining concerts, adventure tourism, and holiday travel momentum at the iconic Victoria Falls.

logistics.transportInfo: Reachable by taxi, rideshare, or walking from nearby hotels.

faqs.question: Are camping passes available?
faqs.answer: Yes, on-site camping is included with some passes.

faqs.question: Are activities included?
faqs.answer: Adventure activities are sold separately.

name: Timkat Festival
shortDescription: Ethiopia's spectacular Epiphany pilgrimage.
description: Ethiopian Orthodox Epiphany celebration featuring spectacular processions of the Tabot, mass baptisms, drumming, and white-robed pilgrims at Fasilides Bath.

logistics.transportInfo: Reachable by taxi, rideshare, or walking from nearby hotels.

faqs.question: Can tourists attend?
faqs.answer: Yes, visitors are warmly welcomed.

faqs.question: Is photography allowed?
faqs.answer: Yes, but respectfully during ceremonies.

name: Ouidah Voodoo Festival
shortDescription: West Africa's iconic Vodun celebration.
description: Benin's most colourful spiritual festival celebrating Vodun religion with drumming, dancing, ceremonies, and vibrant processions drawing diaspora from Brazil and the Caribbean.

logistics.transportInfo: Reachable by taxi, rideshare, or walking from nearby hotels.

faqs.question: Is it open to outsiders?
faqs.answer: Yes, all visitors are welcome.

faqs.question: Is photography allowed?
faqs.answer: Ask before photographing ritual moments.

name: Durbar Festival — Eid al-Fitr
shortDescription: Royal cavalry charge marking Eid in Kano.
description: Royal horseback procession marking the end of Ramadan. Emirs ride at the head of cavalry, trumpeters fill the air, and riders charge in formation before the palace gates.

logistics.transportInfo: Reachable by taxi, rideshare, or walking from nearby hotels.

faqs.question: Is there reserved seating?
faqs.answer: Yes, VIP grandstands can be booked.

faqs.question: Best vantage point?
faqs.answer: The palace gates offer the best view.

name: Homowo Festival
shortDescription: Ga people's joyous harvest celebration.
description: Harvest festival of the Ga people meaning 'hooting at hunger.' Features sacred kpokpoi cooking, ancestral libations, drumming, singing, and joyful parades led by chiefs and priests.

logistics.transportInfo: Reachable by taxi, rideshare, or walking from nearby hotels.

faqs.question: Can I taste kpokpoi?
faqs.answer: Yes, it's offered freely during the festival.

faqs.question: When is the main parade?
faqs.answer: The grand parade is on the opening Saturday.

name: PANAFEST — Pan African Historical Theatre Festival
shortDescription: Pan-African heritage at the slave forts.
description: Ghana's biggest cultural gathering celebrating Pan-African heritage. Features durbar of chiefs at Elmina Castle, atonement rituals, naming ceremonies, and memorial services at slave trade sites.

logistics.transportInfo: Reachable by taxi, rideshare, or walking from nearby hotels.

faqs.question: Is the durbar open to all?
faqs.answer: Yes, but VIP seating is reserved.

faqs.question: Are tours included?
faqs.answer: Castle tours are included with full passes.

name: Gerewol Festival
shortDescription: Wodaabe male beauty contest in the Sahel.
description: Spectacular Wodaabe male beauty and courtship contest. Men paint faces elaborately and perform the Yaake dance. Also features camel racing and traditional feasting.

logistics.transportInfo: Reachable by taxi, rideshare, or walking from nearby hotels.

faqs.question: Is camping available?
faqs.answer: Yes, tented camps are arranged for visitors.

faqs.question: Best month?
faqs.answer: Always early October, after the rains.

name: Eyo Festival
shortDescription: White-robed masquerade parade in Lagos.
description: Iconic Yoruba masquerade festival where thousands of Eyo masqueraders in white robes and tall hats parade through Lagos Island streets honouring Yoruba royalty and the deceased.

logistics.transportInfo: Reachable by taxi, rideshare, or walking from nearby hotels.

faqs.question: Can women dress as Eyo?
faqs.answer: No, only men traditionally participate.

faqs.question: Is it free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the public parade is free.

name: Calabar Carnival
shortDescription: Africa's Biggest Street Party.
description: A month-long celebration featuring carnival parades, cultural displays, music, dance, beauty pageants, and food from Nigeria's diverse ethnic groups.

logistics.transportInfo: Reachable by taxi, rideshare, or walking from nearby hotels.

faqs.question: When is the main parade?
faqs.answer: December 26th–30th annually.

faqs.question: Are grandstands available?
faqs.answer: Yes, paid seating along the route.

name: FESPACO — Pan-African Film Festival
shortDescription: Africa's biggest film festival.
description: Africa's largest film festival showcasing films reflecting diverse African cultures and stories. A major platform for continental filmmakers, artists, and cultural exchange.

logistics.transportInfo: Reachable by taxi, rideshare, or walking from nearby hotels.

faqs.question: Are screenings free?
faqs.answer: Some are free; premieres require passes.

faqs.question: How often is it held?
faqs.answer: Biennial, in odd years.

name: FESTIMA — Festival International des Masques et des Arts
shortDescription: West Africa's largest mask festival.
description: West Africa's largest celebration of traditional mask cultures bringing together over 50 ethnic groups from Burkina Faso, Mali, Benin, Côte d'Ivoire, Togo, and Senegal.

logistics.fullAddress: Dédougou, Boucle du Mouhoun, Burkina Faso
logistics.transportInfo: Reachable by taxi, rideshare, or walking from nearby hotels.

faqs.question: Is photography allowed?
faqs.answer: Yes, but respect dancer requests.

faqs.question: Are guided tours offered?
faqs.answer: Yes, by ASAMA volunteers.

name: Fes Festival of World Sacred Music
shortDescription: Spiritual music inside the Fes medina.
description: Global musicians perform sacred and spiritual music in the historic medina venues of Fes. One of Morocco's most important cultural gatherings blending faith, heritage, and world music.

logistics.transportInfo: Reachable by taxi, rideshare, or walking from nearby hotels.

faqs.question: Are passes needed?
faqs.answer: Yes, evening concerts are ticketed.

faqs.question: Are dress codes enforced?
faqs.answer: Modest dress is appreciated.

name: Gnaoua World Music Festival
shortDescription: Healing trance music by the Atlantic.
description: Celebrates the spiritual healing music of the Gnaoua people alongside world music artists. Essaouira transforms into a vibrant hub with concerts, workshops, and street performances.

logistics.transportInfo: Reachable by taxi, rideshare, or walking from nearby hotels.

faqs.question: Is the festival free?
faqs.answer: Most main stages are free; some are paid.

faqs.question: Can I attend lila ceremonies?
faqs.answer: Yes, all-night ceremonies welcome respectful guests.

name: Lake of Stars Festival
shortDescription: Lakeside global music gathering in Malawi.
description: International 3-day music festival on the shores of Lake Malawi blending local Malawian musicians with international bands, DJs, talks, poetry, film, and wellness activities.

logistics.transportInfo: Reachable by taxi, rideshare, or walking from nearby hotels.

faqs.question: Is camping included?
faqs.answer: Yes, with festival passes.

faqs.question: Is swimming safe?
faqs.answer: Yes, the lake is bilharzia-treated in festival areas.

name: Umhlanga Reed Dance
shortDescription: Eswatini's royal reed-dance pageantry.
description: Annual royal ceremony where thousands of unmarried Swazi girls present freshly cut reeds to the Queen Mother. Features royal dance attended by the King, traditional beadwork, and national cultural pride.

logistics.transportInfo: Reachable by taxi, rideshare, or walking from nearby hotels.

faqs.question: Can tourists attend?
faqs.answer: Yes, with respectful conduct.

faqs.question: Is photography allowed?
faqs.answer: Yes, but not of the King without permission.
